title: Household chlorination: concentration + contact time dosing
context: Household chlorination dosing (concentration, contact time) is a HIGH-risk topic: an incorrect concentration can either fail to make water safe or harm the person consuming it. This unit is hard-gated on a secured WASH/EH expert reviewer (partner-014) and the CI HIGH-risk gate (ci-018). Where authoritative sources differ on a dosing value, the divergence must be stated and both linked rather than averaged. No brand or product recommendations. Builds on safe water storage content (content-008).
objective: Author, source-cite, independently numeric-verify, and WASH/EH-expert-confirm a WashUnit covering household chlorination: the appropriate chlorine concentration, contact time, dosing for different water volumes, safe handling of chlorine, and when to test/verify — with every numeric value tied to a specific authoritative source, independently second-checked, and confirmed by the WASH/EH expert; includes the required disclaimer.
acceptanceCriteria: Every numeric value (chlorine concentration, contact time, dosing for volume, residual targets) is tied to a specific named authoritative source (WHO/UNICEF/CDC) with retrieval date and verified license

Every numeric value is independently second-checked by a person other than the author (numericClaims[].verifiedBy is distinct from the author) and confirmed by the secured WASH/EH expert (expertConfirmed:true on every numericClaim)

Where authoritative sources materially diverge on a dosing value or contact time, the divergence is explicitly stated in the unit and both sources are linked; no averaging of conflicting safety-critical values
A recorded expertSignOff from the secured WASH/EH professional (partner-014) is attached to this content version before merge; the sign-off is version-scoped
Unit includes safe-handling cautions for chlorine (storage, handling, mixing); no brand or product recommendations
Unit carries a persistent 'This is general information, not a substitute for trained WASH practice; seek qualified guidance for uncertainty' disclaimer in the rendered output
All source text is paraphrased — no verbatim copied text; no logos/emblems; CI copy/similarity check passes
CI HIGH-risk gate (ci-018) passes: expertSignOff present, all numericClaims have verifiedBy and expertConfirmed:true, disclaimer field present
Reviewed and approved by a WASH/public-health domain reviewer distinct from the author and the expert; PR-tied review-log entry written
output: A committed, WASH/EH-expert-signed-off, independently numeric-verified, source-cited WashUnit for household chlorination dosing, with recorded expertSignOff, all numericClaims verified and confirmed, divergence stated where sources differ, and the required disclaimer.
